task_completion.description: Verify that a custom dashboard has been created and saved in Splunk.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Splunk 'Dashboards' page showing a newly created dashboard in the list, or the active view of a newly created dashboard in the Splunk Web UI.

task_completion.description: Verify that the dashboard queries the 'security_logs' index.
task_completion.visual_evidence: SPL queries visible in the dashboard's panel edit mode, inline search bar, or source XML showing 'index=security_logs'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the dashboard contains at least 3 distinct visualization panels.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The dashboard view displaying 3 or more separate panels (which should correspond to failed login trends, top attacking source IPs, and most targeted user accounts).

task_completion.description: Verify that a scheduled alert has been created for high-volume attacks.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Splunk 'Alerts' page showing a newly created alert, or the 'Save As Alert' dialog with the 'Schedule' option selected (e.g., Run on Cron Schedule or Run every X minutes).

task_completion.description: Verify that the alert is configured with a threshold-based trigger condition.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Alert configuration settings showing a trigger condition based on volume, such as 'Trigger alert when' set to 'Number of Results' 'is greater than' a specific numeric value.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the Splunk Web UI rather than just writing configuration files in a terminal.
integrity.visual_evidence: A web browser open to http://localhost:8000 displaying the genuine Splunk interface.

integrity.description: Verify the dashboard panels are genuine Splunk visualizations and not mocked up text.
integrity.visual_evidence: Standard Splunk UI elements for panels (e.g., charts, tables, or single value displays) with Splunk's native formatting and editing controls.

integrity.description: Verify the alert was created using the actual Splunk interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: The native Splunk 'Save As Alert' modal or the Splunk Alert settings page, rather than a text editor modifying conf files.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target index for all dashboard queries must be exactly 'security_logs'. The dashboard must contain a minimum of 3 panels. The alert must be a scheduled alert (not real-time) and must explicitly include a numeric threshold condition (e.g., trigger if number of events > X).
